title: قرار وزاري رقم 40: بشأن المعايير الوطنية لخدمة الرعاية الصحية المنزلية

provisions.title: صدربتاريخ:06/03/2024
provisions.content: ينشرهذا القرار فيالجريدةالرسميةويعمل بهاعتباراً مناليوم التاليلتاريخنشره.صدربتاريخ:06/03/2024عبدالرحمنبن محمدالعويسوزيرالصحة ووقايةالمجتمعنشرهذاالقرارالوزاريفي عددالجريدةالرسمية رقم772.ص29.مرفقالقرارالوزاري رقم(40)لسنة2024بشأنالمعاييرالوطنيةلخدمةالرعايةالصحيةالمنزليةNATIONALSTANDARDS FOR HOME HEALTHCARE SERVICESPurpose:Ensurehigh quality, safe and accessible home healthcare services provided byhealthcare facilities and health care professionals.Scope:Thisstandard applies to:■All healthcare facilities licensed by concerned health authorities to providehome healthcare services.•Healthcare Professionals licensed by concerned health authorities to implementhome healthcare services.Definitionsand Abbreviations:Peopleof Determination : people with personal condition or situation that couldmake it difficult for them to participate fully or with limited approach inself- health care. Itincludesindividuals with disabilities such as (physical, intellectual, or sensory), agerelated (either elderly or very young), trauma or medications/drugsrelated.Healthcareprofessional : a licensed healthcare personal working in healthcarefacilities and required to be licensed as per the applicable laws in UnitedArabEmirates.HealthServices : services provided by health care provider to individuals forthe purpose of improving or maintaining a person’s health status.HomeHealth Care Services : Healthcare is a medical, dental or nursing serviceor any supportive care provided by professional caregiver such as but notlimited to physiotherapy, social, occupational therapy, psychologistcounselling, andpalliativecare in the individual or client in their residences to promote, restoreand maintain a person maximum level of comfort, physical and mental functioning,and health status.Licensure :issuing a license to operate a health facility to an individual, government,corporation, partnership, Limited Liability Company (LLC), or other form ofbusiness operation legally responsible for the facility’s operation.Patient :A person who is served by or uses the services of a licensed HomeHealthCare provider.PatientCare Plan : An individualized plan of care devised through aninterdisciplinary team with supporting documentation for each patient.InterdisciplinaryTeam : Consists of various licensed medical Health specialties.Memberscollaborate to ensure that the treatment plan is appropriately applied accordingto the recommendations of the Referring Physician.RegisteredNurse (RN) : Licensed healthcare professional by health authorityasRegistered Nurse.AssistantNurse (AN) : Licensed healthcare professional by health authority asAssistantNurse who provides basic care and assistance to patients under the supervisionof a registered nurses and other healthcare professionals.DentalAssistant : Licensed healthcare professional by health authority as DentalAssistant who provides basic care and assistance to patients under thesupervision of a registered dentist and other healthcare professionals.Physiotherapist(PT) : Licensed healthcare professional by health authority asPhysiotherapist.RespiratoryTherapist(RT) : Licensed healthcareprofessional as by health authority as respiratory therapist.SpeechTherapist (ST) : Licensed healthcare professional by health authority asspeech therapist.OccupationalTherapist(OT) : Licensed Healthcareprofessional by health authority as Occupational Therapist.TreatingPhysician : Licensed Physician who has an established history of treatingspecific medical conditions. Provide consultations, treat patients, usediagnostic tests, refer patients to additional medical expertise and generatemedical reports.TreatingDentist : Licensed Dentist who has an established history of treatingspecific teeth and gums related conditions. Provide consultations, treatpatients, use diagnostic tests, refer patients to additional medical expertiseand generate medical reports.ReferringPhysician : A Physician located at the Referring Healthcare Provider whoreviews the patient's follow-up healthcare plan initiated by the TreatingPhysician, fill a form based on the patient’s medical condition to specifythe necessary home health care services, type, timeframe, frequency. However,the treating physician can be the referring physician.ReferringDentist : A Dentist located at the Referring Healthcare Provider whoreviews the patient's follow-up healthcare plan initiated by the TreatingDentist, fill a form based on the patient’s medical condition to specifythe necessary health care services, type, timeframe, frequency. However, thetreating Dentist can be the referring Dentist.Dietician :Licensed health professionals by health authority that assess, diagnose, andtreat dietary and nutritional problems at an individual and wider public-healthlevel.Palliativecare : is an interdisciplinary medical caregiving approach aimed atoptimizing quality of life and mitigating suffering among people with serious,complex, and often terminal illnesses.informedConsent : a process of communication between a person and a physician orother healthcare professional that results in the person’s authorizationor agreement to undergo a specific medical intervention. It includes theprinciple that a physician has a duty to inform his or her patients about thenature of a proposed or alternative treatment, procedure, test, or research,including the risks and benefits ofeachalternative and of not receiving it. An informed patient can then make achoicewhichprocedure, if any, to undergo.LicensedHealthcare Professionals : a natural person engaged in a healthcareprofession holding a License duly issued by the health authority licensing boardin accordance with the Healthcare Professionals Regulation and the applicableRules, Standards and Policies.1Registration and LicensureRequirements:Allhealthcare facilities providing home healthcare services must comply to thefollowing requirement for licensure:1.1Submit a request of:1.1.1A new facility under the category of “new facility licenseapplication”1.1.2An existing facility under category of “Add service."1.2Home healthcare services is only available for the following healthcare facilitytypes:1.2.1Hospital.1.2.2Centre (Day Care surgery / Medical /Dental/Dialysis Centre /Rehabilitation).1.2.3Clinic (General, Specialized).1.2.4Standalone Home Healthcare Facility.1.3Add the service under their facility trade license.1.4Ensure that all the home care service provided by the facility must be withinthe scope of the facility license.1.5Ensure the availability of all medical equipment needed to provide theservice.1.6Ensure that all healthcare professionals employed by the facility are licensedto provide competency based or privileged health services at home.1.7Employ a sufficient number of qualified and licensed healthcare professionals tosatisfy the service requirements and to meet patient’s needs for properservices provision with minimum requirement of one (1) Physician and three (3)Registered Nurses and (1) dentist and (1) dental assistance and an appropriateskill mix of staff including physiotherapist, occupational therapist, speechtherapist, respiratory therapist,anddietician Social Worker and Psychologist.2Healthcare Professional Minimum Requirements2.1Home Healthcare services must have a Director (A physician, dentist or aregistered nurse) who shall act as a supervisor over the professional staff, beresponsible for the services provided in the home healthcare facility andestablish the services policies and procedures.2.21 full time Nurse Clinical Educator - Licensed Registered Nurse preferablywithgraduate level prepared. The clinical educator assumes a leadershipposition to facilitate the professional development of home health nurses andeducate other staff on the organization’s policies, procedures, andexpectations.2.3Quality and performance improvement nurse -responsible for quality andperformance improvement, promotes excellence in clinical practice in theorganization. This is accomplished by assessing, planning, organizing,administering, and coordinatingallaspects of the program. This position can be assigned to any of theexitingnursingstaff.2.4Infection control nurse- The Infection Control Registered Nurse providesadministrative, surveillance and consultative work in planning, developing,coordinating, and administering an Infection Control Program for home carepatients. This position can be assigned to any of the exiting nursingstaff.2.5Healthcare professionals who may work and provide home health servicesare:2.5.1Physician2.5.2Dentist2.5.3Nursing (Nurse Practitioners, Specialty Nurse, Registered Nurses and AssistantNurses under supervision of registered nurse). The ratio of AN to RN should notexceed 1:22.5.4Physiotherapist and physiotherapist assistant.2.5.5Nutritionist and dietitian.2.5.6Psychologist and Social worker (must be licensed by authorities).2.6Other healthcare professionals which are not mentioned above will be handledcase by case.2.7All healthcare professionals must provide care to patients of Home Healthcarefacility within their scope of practice.3Health Facility Design Requirement:Forstandalone facility, should follow the following requirement:3.1The facility must be an independent unit that is not connected to any otherunits or activities.3.2The location of the facility must be easily accessible.3.3The facility must have a sufficient area to accommodate all required services(not less than 60 square meters.4Duties for Healthcare Facilities, Providers and Professionals:4.1Accept referrals from licensed Specialist / Consultant and within the scope ofhis/her specialty.4.2Assess the referred patients within 3 days from receiving a referral.4.3Provide transfer and referral service to emergency or other healthcare specialtywhenever required during the patient care.4.4Home care facility should provide a documented policy and procedures of thefollowing:4.4.1Patient/Relatives’ acceptance criteria, consent form and referral to homehealthcare from a health facility.4.4.2International Patient Safety goals.4.4.3Patient’s assessments and reassessment.4.4.4Patient’s medication management.4.4.5Incident reporting.4.4.6Discharge/transfer policy (if any).4.4.7Provide palliative care.4.4.8Patient record management and retention.4.4.9Patient and family’s rights and responsibilities policy.4.4.10Policy on hazardous waste management (if any).4.4.11Infection control measures.4.4.12Emergency action plan in the office.4.4.13Medical equipment and devices related to the services provided shall bemaintained according to the manufacturer requirements.4.5Clinical care services provided by a licensed physician additional to theirscope of practice are:4.5.1Physician consultations prior to discharging an inpatient from a health facilityto home healthcare facility.4.5.2Physician consultations in the patient’s home.4.5.3Comprehensive assessment (includes medical, functional, and psychological,social, and nutritional assessment), plan of patient care, evaluation,treatment, management and follow ups. (The assessment could be in facility orduring the home visit)4.5.4Support preventive practices for general health and wellness throughidentification of any further needs for skilled nursing care, need of supportivecare or specialized therapy at home.4.5.5Telephonic or telehealth follow up consultation.4.5.6Prescription, replacement, and refill of medications.4.5.7Prescription of specific personal medical devices.4.5.8Home preparation to assess environment and set up equipment whererequired.4.5.9Home visit for prenatal or postnatal evaluation and basic management.4.5.10Home visit for newborn evaluation and management.4.5.11Home visit for complex wound care and debridement.4.5.12Education and counseling, consistent with the type/nature of treatmentand/or care management need.4.5.13Clinical services by dentists for target groups:4.5.13.1Preventive dentistry4-5.13.2Dental emergency4.5.13.3Simple fillings4.5.13.4Scaling4.5.13.5Simple extraction4.5.13.6Remov
